fact: July 18 is exactly 199 days into the year, which means there are only 166 days left until New Year's Eve!
In the Northern Hemisphere, this is peak summer heat season - the sun stays up for over 15 hours in many places, making it perfect for epic water balloon fights!
Cicadas are screaming their loudest around this time of year, creating a buzzing symphony that can reach 120 decibels - louder than a chainsaw!
Why do fireflies blink so much in mid-July? They're having their biggest dating season, flashing secret love codes to find the perfect mate!
In Australia and other Southern Hemisphere countries, July 18 falls smack in the middle of winter, when penguins are huddling together for warmth.
Sunflowers are towering giants right now, some growing so fast they can add 6 inches in a single week during this peak growing period!
The Cancer zodiac season ends around July 22, so July 18 babies are born under the crab sign - known for being super loyal but also a little moody!
Ocean temperatures in many places hit their warmest point about a month after July 18, because water takes forever to heat up compared to air.
Mosquitoes are at their most annoying peak right around this date in many regions - a single female can lay up to 300 eggs at once!
In northern Alaska, the sun barely sets at all around July 18, creating an eerie orange twilight that lasts all night long.
Corn plants are having growth spurts so intense that farmers say you can actually hear them growing on hot July nights - they make tiny popping sounds!
July 18 sits perfectly in the middle of what meteorologists call 'the dog days of summer' - named after the Dog Star Sirius, not because dogs are panting everywhere!
